sections.title: Slide 4: The art and science of prompting

elements.text: The art and science of prompting

content.text: Prompts

are how you ask Copilot 
Chat to do something for you —
like

creating, summarizing, 
reviewing, or researching.

Think about prompting like having 
a conversation, using plain but 
clear language and providing 
context like you would with an 
assistant.

elements.alt: Tell Copilot Chat what you need - Review text, Summarize information, Create engaging content, Research what's happening
Text box background

sections.title: Slide 5: The Art and Science of prompting

elements.text: The Art and Science of prompting

elements.alt: Microsoft 365 Copilot Logo

content.text:
To get the best 
response, it’s 
important to focus 
on some of the

key 
elements

when 
phrasing your 
Copilot prompts.

elements.alt: Include the right prompt ingredients banner

content.text: Goal
Context
Source
Expectations
What

response do 
you want 
from 
Copilot 
Chat?
Why

do 
you need it 
and who is 
involved?
Which

information 
sources or 
samples 
should Copilot 
Chat use?
How

should 
Copilot Chat 
respond
to best meet 
your 
expectations?

elements.caption: Prompt Ingredients
elements.summary: Describes the four key elements of a good prompt: Goal, Context, Source, and Expectations.

elements.alt: Microsoft 365 Copilot Logo
Diagram showing the four prompt ingredients applied to an example prompt

content.text:
Help me write an email to inform the customer 
about a product change.

Since the customer is not a 
global administrator,

I need to reference the URL 
that describes the change,

and provide clear and 
concise information.
Instruction
Tell me about this or do that
Role

an identity that the AI is expected to emulate, act like, or 
duplicate in it’s reply – “act like a…”
Context

show the AI more info about where/what it is – could be data, 
examples, previous articles, etc. “What are video codecs?” vs. 
“Can you create an outline for a 20 minute tutorial to teach 
support engineers about video codecs?”

Tone/Style 
Information

tone of writing – serious, academic, empathetic, inspiring, can 
be “write like this examples”

Formatting 
Information
table, markup language, code, etc.

An Example 
or Examples

give it data to show what you want – the more the better – above 
20 not helpful

elements.caption: Elements of a Great Prompt
elements.summary: Describes instruction, role, context, tone/style information, formatting information, and examples as elements of a great prompt.

sections.title: Slide 25: How to use: Copilot in Teams for chat

elements.text: How to use: Copilot in Teams for chat

content.text: Microsoft Teams Logo, symbol, meaning, history, PNG
Catch up on a chat thread with a summary or by getting key points and action items
Draft a new chat entry in the thread
Rewrite a draft and adjust for length and tone

elements.text: Use the Copilot chat pane to

content.text: Ask questions about the content of the chat – does not work in Channels
Summarize over a period of time
Ask about decisions, open items, tasks
Ask what a specific person said
Ask about links
Click on the Prompt
When creating a chat entry click on the Copilot icon
Guide icon ____ to show the prompts to edit a slide or learn about the presentation
----to show the Rewrite and Adjust prompts when drafting a new chat

content.text: Note the specific prompts shown may vary
Microsoft Teams Logo, symbol, meaning, history, PNG
Get help during a meeting with a summary, key points, sentiment, or potential questions
Catch up on a meeting with a summary or by asking questions
Works during 1:1 and group calls, Meet Now, Scheduled private meetings, recurring meetings, Channel meetings

elements.text: Use the Copilot chat pane to

content.text: Get a summary of the meeting so far
Key discussion points
Who said what or what people said
Where do people disagree
Suggest action items
Click on the Prompt
Guide icon __ to show list of curated prompts

content.text: Use Copilot to Summarize key takeaways, track actions, and highlight decisions from any recorded meetings
Prompt:
“Summarize the last meeting and list all actions items assigned to me.”

content.text: Use Copilot to extract action items from meetings, chats, and posts
so we don’t miss a thing.
Prompt: “What action items were assigned to me this week in this meeting /.”

content.text: When Using Copilot in Teams, specificity helps. It helps Copilot Understand your Intent and generate more relevant content
Prompt: “What did [person] say about the topic []

Decorative background graphic
Two colleagues working together on a laptop

elements.text: What did [person] say about [topic]? in Copilot in Teams

content.text: Try this prompt in your next meeting
What did [person] say about [x]?
Summarize all emails, Teams chats, and meetings involving [Manager’s Name] this week. Highlight key decisions, pending actions, and deadlines.

sections.title: Identify sentiment or concerns in Copilot in Teams

content.text: Try this prompt in your next meeting
List the biggest concern for each participant in the meeting.
Are there any unresolved items or any outstanding open items that need addressing?
Suggest follow-up questions

elements.alt: Screenshot of Copilot in Teams showing questions to ask based on a meeting.
Screenshot of Copilot in Teams showing unresolved questions from a meeting.
Microsoft Copilot logo

content.text: Try this prompt in your next meeting

Summarize key takeaways, decisions, and action items from today’s meeting, identify unresolved topics, and draft a proposed agenda for our next session—then prepare a follow-up message I can send to the team

sections.title: New Feature (forthcoming! Not yet in GCC)

elements.alt: Screenshot of Teams showing the Facilitator feature. Text explains Facilitator is a Teams agent that will automatically generate notes, manage meeting time, and summarize decisions. A menu shows an option to 'Turn on Facilitator'.

content.text: Use Copilot during meetings:
Don’t wait until after
Summarize the discussion so far
Getting real-time info

Highlight a pending question no one answered yet
What decisions were made in this chat?
Use tables and lists:
Ask for a table (options, pros/cons) as mentioned, helps to digest complex discussions

List the questions asked during this meeting & the person that asked the question & who responded.
List any open items that have not been addressed in this meeting. Output in table format.
Prompt Gallery inspiration
Copilot Chat (both M365 + CP Chat) includes a Prompt Gallery

sections.title: Find more Teams prompts to try in Copilot Lab

content.text:
Find action items
Are there any action items for me?

Compare Ideas
For each idea discussed, identify the pros and cons formatted as a table with 3 headers: idea, pros, cons.

What are the options?
Create a table of the options discussed with pros and cons

List ideas
List 5 ideas discussed

Get the schedule
List the key dates in a table

Get clarity
List the different opinions and suggest clarifying questions to ask for each

What decisions were made
Did the team achieve consensus on a decision and what was it?

Be prepared
What are the goals and topics from the meeting? Format each section with a bolded heading, and bolded names

Keep meetings moving
What questions can the group ask to generate more ideas or insights? Limit each to 30-60 characters

Summarize meetings
Summarize what was discussed about
Top 10 things to try first with M365 Copilot: Downloadable Kit
